case: Switzerland d01 reinit-h36 128x128x44 dt=18s force_fp64, RTX 5090
instrument: probe_nsys_steady.py STEADY50 (3rd 50-step call, warmed, no profiler unless noted) or probe_ab_identity hour3/200

rows.note: reproduces the prior sprint's reverted 192.4 regression on this base
capture does NOT rescue niter50: device-bound

rows.nsys: 6728 cuLaunch/step, device busy 112 ms/step (steady win), 10164 kernels/step -> DEVICE-bound
196 cuLaunch/step, 7.3 cuGraphLaunch/step avg 17.9ms (ALWAYS_UPDATE patch cost), tiered field gate PASS

readings: The ONLY strong wall axis is the condensation niter: 50->16 (unrolled) = -70 ms (190->120) regardless of every other knob/flag.

Host-launch removal works mechanically (V3: 13,958->196 launches/step; launch API 159->0.5 ms/step) but the wall is DEVICE-bound at ~112-120 ms in cond16 builds (D nsys), so capture is wall-neutral here.

The prior sprint's 'post-fix device 15.4 ms/step floor' is refuted as a CUPTI-drop undercount: the same-structure cond-unroll builds measure 112-116 ms/step device busy on a clean profile; its '4-7x from host removal' projection inherited that error.
capture-noslice is bitwise-transparent (V5==V2 0/168); the DYNAMIC_SLICE_* command types alone caused the 99/168 numerics flips of V1/V3.
ALWAYS_UPDATE graph patching costs ~17.9 ms per cuGraphLaunch on this program (V3 forensics); NEVER_UPDATE (VMM) removes it but exposes the same device floor.
